name: S1 6.1 - Toilet

sheetComments.author: Kyle Morris
sheetComments.text: Restrooms locks. Hand standpipe still locked. No water.

sheetComments.author: Dagmar Growe

name: S1 38.6 - Toilet
landmark: TH CG W2 (RD>TR) water here (along with toilets etc). Frey Tr ends here at Pueblo Loop Tr., which is very much worth exploring in full to learn about the ruins and history at Bandelier. Before

sheetComments.author: Kyle Morris
sheetComments.text: Water is on

sheetComments.author: Kerry Stewart

sheetComments.author: Aspen Adams

name: S3 28.4 - Toilet
landmark: TH (RD>TR) Trailhead with vault toilet near Upper Canjilon Lakes campground.

name: S3 52.2 - Toilet
landmark: W2 2017. Campground proper (E) reportedly also has faucets and vault toilets, likely seasonal.

sheetComments.author: Jac Mitchell
sheetComments.text: 10/21 all facilities closed for season

sheetComments.author: Will Emmer
sheetComments.text: Sept 2023. Water is off in the campground, but in addition to the lake the campground host told me that he had some water available for hikers and bikers.

sheetComments.author: Aspen Adams
sheetComments.text: Faucets off and pit toilets closed

sheetComments.author: Dagmar Growe
sheetComments.text: Toilets open., but no water at camp ground. Trough is unfilterable! (I think it's blood in there). Go to lake .

sheetComments.author: Alan Tracey
sheetComments.text: Toilets open. Faucet not working and trough is nearly empty

sheetComments.author: Chris Hoffer
sheetComments.text: Trough empty

name: S4 52.9 - Toilet
landmark: to backtrack up to the trailhead at previous waypoint and then follow Crossing (W3) roads south to County Rd B007 / Dunn Bridge crossing of the Rio Grande, 20 miles total, then continue 2 mi farther to the village of Arroyo Hondo at NM Hwy 522. End of trail from Sheep Crossing of Rio Grande. Toilet and table here (no water). (CCW if you find the Rio too dangerous to ford, then you may need to backtrack to Cerro or Questa and take an RTD bus around. Bus stops in village of Arroyo Hondo, then hike or hitch down to familiar

sheetComments.author: Jac Mitchell
sheetComments.text: 10/21 Thigh deep

sheetComments.author: Kyle Morris
sheetComments.text: Crossed downstream of rapids. 180 CFS. Had to swim but current wasn't really noticable.

sheetComments.author: Will Emmer
sheetComments.text: 21 Sep 23. Water quality looked better than anything seen until the Rio San Antonio. See notes about the 7638 well.

sheetComments.author: Will Emmer
sheetComments.text: 21 Sep 23. Crossed upstream of the small rapids at the widest spot where the east side switchbacks start. Only 135 CFS and at this spot I couldn’t feel any current, but ended up still having to swim which was easy to do. In retrospect, you can better see where you can likely just walk across from higher up in the canyon by spotting the darker (less silty) water closer to the rapids to the south (the better crossing point had tiny riffles). This difference wasn’t obvious to see at the water level, as it was from some elevation.

sheetComments.author: Andreas
sheetComments.text: Downsteam gauge was at 4.5 ft and 600 cfs. I crossed at widest point between downstream ripples and upstream ones. Aimed for large boulder where trail comes down on the east side. I had to swim 90% of the width. More difficult than I thought but everything came out unscathed. I'm not a strong swimmer and used my inflatable mattress and pillow as flotation devices for myself and my pack.

sheetComments.author: Kerry Stewart
sheetComments.text: 10/03 Shin deep.

sheetComments.author: Chris Hoffer
sheetComments.text: Shin deep, 50cfs and 1.25’ I think lower than normal for Sep

sheetComments.author: Chris Hoffer
sheetComments.text:
.
Re-opened

name: S5 8.0 - Toilet
landmark: TH (RD>TR) reference on signs. Modern vault toilet. Picnic table(s). Signed Latir Peak Wilderness boundary. Occasional access to Cabresto

name: S6/7 27.0 - Toilet
landmark: San Cristobal (PO L) to postmaster Mike) but ask permission first. Bathroom, water, and trash can also con permiso. A little ways back down the road, along the route, is the modest-looking (but not inexpensive) Taos Gogi Eco Lodge in a bucolic setting, with occasional rooms available on short notice. An RTD Blue Bus stop is also within reach, with daily runs to Taos south and Questa north. (c) Brett Tucker CCW = counter-clockwise (fall hike); JL / JR = junction, turn left or right (for CW travel along the route, reverse for CCW); XR = cross road; XT = cross trail; !! = take note SE CW CCW Shown on Mapset. Shading icon color. Carsonite post in large cairn at top of switchbacks. End of Goose Creek Tr 65 at nexus of trails. Somewhat confusing on map, and only the main route ahead (W) is really visible on the ground here above treeline. This S6 9.6 TJ (S6A START)

sheetComments.author: Jac Mitchell
sheetComments.text: 10/21 Didn't stop to ask postmaster Mike for water

name: S7 16.8 - Toilet
landmark: !! XR (END XC) 64 into Rio Grande Gorge Bridge rest area. Your extended stint of xc travel ends here. West Rim TH / Gorge rest area. Developed water (drinking fountains) 24/7. Restrooms closed after 5pm. Shade ramadas. Didn’t see vending.

name: S7 28.8 - Toilet
landmark: Taos Jct bridge W3 Orilla Verde Campground as well as a boat launch / take-out with vault toilet. Picuris Trail TH. Good flowing spring at roadside (6-2020) is 0.25W.

sheetComments.author: Jac Mitchell
sheetComments.text: 9/21 all W3's have had water thus far due to recent heavy precipitation/also just being a generally reliable source, I imagine.

sheetComments.author: Kyle Morris
sheetComments.text: Water hydrant at campground is on

sheetComments.author: Brian Tanzman
sheetComments.text: Spigots on. Up ahead where the Picarus trail ends at the dirt road is a nice primitive campsite, $5. There's another one down the road a bit too. The self pay station is back towards the bridge though so it's kinda a pain to pick an open site and then backtrack. There's a camp host nearby so you need to pay. #1 is a nice quiet site next to the trailhead and surrounded by poison ivy and sumac but avoidable when setting up tent. I mention all this if you need a protected place to camp like we did in thunderstorms, not a lot of other options.

sheetComments.author: Molly Katzman
sheetComments.text: Water spigots on at campground. I should've read Buck 30 and Kyle's comments before collecting from roadside spring which was awkward to collect from and flowing slowly.

sheetComments.author: Chris Hoffer
sheetComments.text: Water at the developed campground. The primitive sites are great for camping in this beautiful area before/after Ranchos de Taos

name: S7 30.0 - Toilet
landmark: W1-2 Light spring flow across Slide Trail among willows 6-20 Upper trailhead for Slide Trail. Popular spot among mtb'ers using new trail network in area (not the Slide Trail so much). Vault toilet here. CW head E on the paved county road (110). You're finally out of the vast Rio

sheetComments.author: Jac Mitchell
sheetComments.text: 9/21 light flow across trail

sheetComments.author: Mike Unger
sheetComments.text: Very light flow. It would be difficult to scoop.

sheetComments.author: Mike Unger
sheetComments.text: Very small flow. Difficult to scoop.

sheetComments.author: Kyle Morris
sheetComments.text: Still flowing.

sheetComments.author: Will Emmer
sheetComments.text: Oct 2023. Just a trickle. Rio Pueblo at 29.3 miles was easily accessible as well.

sheetComments.author: Andreas
sheetComments.text: Hardly anything. I passed over not thinking that it could be the spring

sheetComments.author: Kerry Stewart
sheetComments.text: Flowing.

sheetComments.author: Molly Katzman
sheetComments.text: 9/7 Just a trickle, would've been difficult to collect.

name: S7 30.7 - Toilet
landmark: TH JL (TR>RD) Grande del Norte National Monument for good, along with public lands between here and your upcoming town stop at Ranchos de Taos, so no camping allowed ahead. (CCW note that you want to join the Slide Trail here from the TH, which heads N just W of the toilet.) You don't want the road heading due N here, but rather the lesser utility

name: S8 23.3 - Toilet
landmark: Waypoint S8 23.3 N) on highway (parking and vault toilet across the road). Be careful with traffic on this narrow winding 2-lane road. (c) Brett Tucker CCW = counter-clockwise (fall hike); JL / JR = junction, turn left or right (for CW travel along the route, reverse for CCW); XR = cross road; XT = cross trail; !! = take note SE CW CCW Shown on Mapset. Shading icon color. It's a mere half mile off-route to the laid-back Sipapu Ski & Summer Resort. True to its name, Sipapu offers year-round services, namely a motel and cabins, restaurant / grill, and something of a convenience- store level grocery option. Sometimes busy, other times a virtual ghost town, Sipapu has agreed to hold hiker packages in the past but call Sipapu resort (M L ahead to confirm current arrangements to ensure a smooth resupply S8 36.15398, -105.54773 8193' maildrop) here. Information has historically been as follows: UPS deliveries only to Sipapu Ski & Summer Resort, Attn Lisa (Please Hold for -hiker's name here-), 5224 Hwy 518, Vadito NM 87579. Contingent upon overnight stay. CALL FIRST 800-587-2240 www.sipapunm.com Or just stop by in hopes of enjoying a quick burger and beer on the back patio built along the banks of the Rio Pueblo. S of paved NM 518, bridged crossing of Rio Pueblo along entrance road

sheetComments.author: Kyle Morris
sheetComments.text: The restaurant is only open on the weekends currently.

sheetComments.author: Andreas
sheetComments.text: Also easy access to water across street at picnic area

sheetComments.author: Molly Katzman
sheetComments.text: Restaurant closed for the season after Labor Day, but snacks and drinks still sold at the store.
